metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a CLI proving command for the chooser shape in AC1, e.g.: `python3 -c "import yaml,sys; c=yaml.safe_load(open('.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml')); assert c['blank_issues_enabled']==False; assert len(c.get('contact_links',[]))==2" && ls .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/*.yml | wc -l | grep -q 3`
findings.finding: AC1's second half — 'the rendered new-issue chooser shows 3 forms + 2 contact links and no blank-issue option' — has no proving command; it relies on a manual visual inspection with no deterministic CLI equivalent, making it inconsistent with the rest of the plan's command-level evidence standard.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the visual-check prose with: `python3 -c "import yaml; c=yaml.safe_load(open('.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml')); assert c['blank_issues_enabled'] is False; assert len(c['contact_links']) == 2" && ls .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/[0-9]*.yml | wc -l | grep -q '^3$'`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit proving commands for AC2, e.g.: `grep -c 'required: true' .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/00-bug.yml | grep -q '^3$'` and `for f in .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/[0-9]*.yml; do grep -q 'labels:' "$f" || exit 1; done`
findings.finding: AC2 describes required file contents ('contains `render: shell` and three `validations: required: true` blocks; all forms declare their `labels:`') but provides no concrete proving command, making it inconsistent with every other AC which supplies an explicit grep or gh CLI call.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: `grep -c 'required: true' .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/00-bug.yml | grep -q '^3$' && grep -q 'render: shell' .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/00-bug.yml && for f in .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/[0-9]*.yml; do grep -q 'labels:' "$f" || (echo "missing labels in $f" && exit 1); done`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC4's proving command to also assert `blank_issues_enabled: false`, e.g.: `grep -q 'blank_issues_enabled: false' .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml && grep -c 'url:' .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml | grep -qE '^[2-9]'`
findings.finding: AC4's proving command (`grep -c "url:" .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml` ≥ 2) verifies the URL count but does NOT verify `blank_issues_enabled: false` — that half of the AC has no proving command, leaving a silent gap.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC4 prove line with: `grep -q 'blank_issues_enabled: false' .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml && grep -c 'url:' .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml | grep -qE '^[2-9]$'`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the AC6 grep with a more specific anchor, e.g.: `grep -qi 'discussions.*off\|discussions.*disabled\|not.*discussions\|issues.*not.*discussions' SUPPORT.md` or require a specific prose marker like `<!-- discussions: off -->` that the grep can anchor on.
findings.finding: AC6's proving command `grep -qi "discussions" SUPPORT.md` is a substring false-positive (defect taxonomy item 1): any line containing the word 'discussions' — including one that says 'We considered Discussions but rejected them' — would satisfy the grep, without verifying the three specific claims (discussions are off, questions go to issues, revisit trigger is stated).
findings.suggested_fix: Add a required prose marker in SUPPORT.md (e.g., a comment or a section heading `## Discussions` with canonical text) and prove with: `grep -q 'Discussions are disabled' SUPPORT.md && grep -q 'revisit' SUPPORT.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC #6 with the SUPPORT.md work description: either add an explicit instruction in the What/SUPPORT.md bullet to include a one-line Discussions-off statement (and update Scope to match), or change the AC #6 verify target to a file that IS described as receiving that content (e.g., a DECISIONS.md, the repo README, or a new prose section in SUPPORT.md explicitly added to scope).
findings.finding: AC #6's verify step (`grep -qi "discussions" SUPPORT.md`) tests for Discussions-off content in SUPPORT.md, but the What section's SUPPORT.md instructions never direct the implementer to add a Discussions statement there — the Scope also bounds SUPPORT.md work to 'routing sections only'. This is an orphan criterion: the AC requires file content that no described task produces.
findings.location: AC line 6 vs. What section (SUPPORT.md sub-bullet) + Scope (IN clause)
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the What/SUPPORT.md bullet: 'Add one sentence stating Discussions are off, questions go to Issues, and the revisit trigger (question-issues clogging the tracker).' Then expand Scope IN to 'SUPPORT.md rework (routing sections + Discussions-off notice)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC #5 (or add a sub-bullet) to cover the bug/enhancement existence check: e.g., append 'and `bug`, `enhancement` to the `gh label list` assertion' so all five labels referenced in the forms have a covering criterion.
findings.finding: The What section describes verifying that `bug` and `enhancement` labels already exist as part of the labels step, but no Acceptance Criterion covers this verification — it is uncovered described work.
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC #5 verify to: `gh label list` includes needs-triage, waiting-for-info, question, bug, enhancement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before renaming templates, grep CONTRIBUTING.md, README, docs, and any known external references for `?template=bug_report.yml` and `?template=feature_request.yml`; update or remove those links as part of this PR, and add a one-line note in the PR description confirming no live references remain (the rollback plan is: git revert the rename commit).
findings.finding: The plan renames existing template files (bug_report.yml → 00-bug.yml, feature_request.yml → 10-feature.yml) but does not acknowledge that any external documentation, contributor guides, or deeplinks using the `?template=bug_report.yml` query-string convention will silently break, and provides no rollback/redirect path for those references.
findings.location: What section — '.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ rework'; plan phrase 'rework in place'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit step to the 'What' section: 'Grep repo for `?template=bug_report.yml` and `?template=feature_request.yml`; update any hits to the new names. Rollback: `git revert <rename-commit>` and re-enable old filenames.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify `20-question.yml` form fields — at minimum: a body/description textarea with a placeholder guiding the user (e.g., 'What are you trying to do? What have you already tried?') and clarify whether any fields are required.
findings.finding: The `20-question.yml` form interaction surface is underspecified: the plan names the form and its label but provides no fields, prompts, required/optional designations, or guiding text, unlike the bug and feature forms which are fully specced. An implementer must invent the question form's content.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the `20-question.yml` bullet: one optional `textarea` id: description, label: 'Your question', placeholder: 'What are you trying to do? What have you already tried? Rebar version and OS if relevant.' — keep all fields optional so the form is truly low-friction.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `waiting-for-info` has a current consumer in this plan's scope, OR defer its creation to the stale-close automation epic where it will actually be used.
findings.finding: The `waiting-for-info` label has no current consumer in this plan's scope: no issue form auto-applies it, no automation references it, and stale-close automation is explicitly deferred out of scope. Creating it now is YAGNI — it is part of 'Go's label state machine, scaled down' but the scaled-down version the plan actually delivers doesn't use it.
findings.location: What / Labels section: 'Create labels: needs-triage, waiting-for-info, question'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `waiting-for-info` from the label creation list in this plan and add it to the deferred stale-bot automation epic. The three labels that DO have immediate consumers are `needs-triage` (applied by both 00-bug.yml and 10-feature.yml), `question` (applied by 20-question.yml), and verification of `bug`/`enhancement`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the AC proof for `render: shell` to specify the field ID: e.g., `grep -A2 'id: environment' 00-bug.yml | grep -q 'render: shell'`, and confirm the environment field is also `required: true` per the plan's intent.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the bug form's environment textarea will have `render: shell`, but the existing bug_report.yml places `render: shell` only on the logs field (line 74), not on the environment field (line 64–68, which has no render attribute). The AC proof string ('00-bug.yml contains render: shell') already passes against the current file for the wrong field, so the criterion cannot distinguish a compliant implementation from the status quo.
findings.location: What section ('required environment textarea … render: shell') / AC line: '00-bug.yml contains render: shell and three validations: required: true blocks'
findings.suggested_fix: In the bug form spec and AC, call out that `render: shell` belongs on the `environment` field (not just anywhere in the file), and add `required: true` to the environment field. Update the AC proof to: `grep -A3 'id: environment' .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/00-bug.yml | grep -q 'render: shell'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a `.github/labels.yml` (or equivalent) that declares all required labels (bug, enhancement, needs-triage, waiting-for-info, question) so the 'verify' assertion is codebase-grounded, or explicitly expand the AC proof to include `gh label list` checks for bug and enhancement.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'verify bug/enhancement exist' as a label-creation step, but no label definition file (e.g., .github/labels.yml) exists anywhere in the repo. Labels are live GitHub API state, not file-backed, so there is no in-repo artifact to probe and the 'verify' claim is unverifiable from the codebase.
findings.location: What section: 'Create labels … and verify bug/enhancement exist'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `.github/labels.yml` file listing all five labels with colors/descriptions, and update the AC to validate all five with a single `gh label list` check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the plan (and tighten the AC) that the existing Documentation and Support contact_links will be removed from config.yml, leaving exactly the two specified links; update the AC proof to `grep -c "url:" .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml` equals 2 (not ≥ 2).
findings.finding: The plan's What section specifies that config.yml contact_links will contain exactly two entries (Gerrit and Security), but the current config.yml already has four contact_links (Documentation, Gerrit/CONTRIBUTING, Security, Support). The plan is silent about what happens to the existing Documentation and Support links — whether they are removed, retained, or repurposed — leaving a gap between the specified and actual post-implementation state.
findings.location: What section: 'config.yml … contact_links: (a) Gerrit … (b) Security' / AC: 'grep -c "url:" .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml ≥ 2'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC proof to `[ "$(grep -c 'url:' .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml)" = "2" ]` and add a 'remove existing Documentation/Support contact_links' step to the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `pyproject.toml` `[project.urls] Issues` to the Scope IN list and update its value from the SUPPORT.md path to `https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/issues` as part of this plan.
findings.finding: pyproject.toml's [project.urls] Issues entry points to SUPPORT.md (not to the GitHub Issues tracker) and is an unenumerated consumer: enabling GitHub Issues makes the PyPI-published Issues URL stale — PyPI users clicking 'Report Issues' from the package page will land on SUPPORT.md instead of the live issue tracker.
findings.suggested_fix: Change pyproject.toml line 77 to: `Issues = "https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/issues"` and add pyproject.toml to the Scope IN list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md` to Scope IN (or acknowledge it as an out-of-scope known consumer) and update its mirror-hygiene steps to remove or annotate the `has_issues=false` line so it no longer conflicts with the intentional Issues-enabled state.
findings.finding: infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md contains an explicit 'mirror-hygiene' step (`gh api -X PATCH /repos/navapbc/rebar -F has_issues=false`) that would silently re-disable Issues if a maintainer runs it after this plan lands; this runbook is an unenumerated consumer of the has_issues state.
findings.location: infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md lines 118-119 and 203-204 / What section (has_issues flip)
findings.suggested_fix: In the mirror-lock runbook's hygiene section, replace `-F has_issues=false` with a comment noting that Issues are intentionally enabled per this plan, or remove that flag entirely from the hygiene script.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the What section that bug_report.yml and feature_request.yml must be DELETED (not just replaced) and add an AC verification step that the old filenames no longer exist in .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/.
findings.finding: The plan says 'rework in place' the existing bug_report.yml and feature_request.yml into 00-bug.yml and 10-feature.yml, but these are actually file renames (delete + create), not in-place edits — GitHub file rename semantics mean the old filenames vanish. Any external documentation or deep links to the old filenames (e.g. in-form markdown links that reference the template by name, or cached/bookmarked URLs) will break silently.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Delete bug_report.yml and feature_request.yml; create 00-bug.yml and 10-feature.yml.' Add AC: `ls .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ | grep -v '00-bug\|10-feature\|20-question\|config'` returns nothing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the What section an explicit requirement to remove (or replace with a positive Gerrit-for-code-changes note) the 'Issues may be disabled' markdown preamble from both reworked form files.
findings.finding: Both existing issue form files (bug_report.yml, feature_request.yml) contain an in-form markdown note stating 'Issues may be disabled — see SUPPORT.md', which will be stale and misleading once Issues are enabled. The plan's 'rework in place' instruction doesn't explicitly call out removing or updating this stale mirror-disabled disclaimer.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section under 00-bug.yml and 10-feature.yml, add: 'Remove the existing markdown preamble warning that Issues may be disabled; replace with a one-line Gerrit redirect: "For code contributions, see CONTRIBUTING.md — Gerrit is the review flow."'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief rationale in the `config.yml` bullet for why the existing 'Documentation' and 'Get support → SUPPORT.md' contact links are removed (or explicitly retain one of them), so the implementer knows the reduction to 2 links is intentional and users landing on the chooser have orientation cues.
findings.finding: The `config.yml` rework specifies exactly 2 contact_links (Gerrit + Security), replacing the existing 4 (which include 'Documentation & command reference' and 'Get support → SUPPORT.md'), but the plan states no positive rationale for *removing* those two orientation-focused links — only rationale for adding the two new ones. An implementer has no basis to know whether the removal is intentional or an oversight, and a new user landing on the issue chooser loses the direct path to docs and support.
findings.location: What section — config.yml bullet: 'contact_links: (a) Gerrit … (b) Security'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the config.yml bullet, e.g.: 'Drop the existing docs and SUPPORT.md contact links — after the SUPPORT.md rework the Issues tab itself surfaces docs references, and the chooser's primary job is routing code contributors (→ Gerrit) and security reporters (→ SECURITY.md); general orientation is handled by SUPPORT.md, not the chooser.' Alternatively, retain a third link pointing to SUPPORT.md for user orientation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add auth preconditions to the plan: state that `PATCH /repos/navapbc/rebar` requires a GitHub token with `repo` scope held by a repository admin, and that the `gh` CLI must be authenticated under that identity before running the AC1 proof command.
findings.finding: The plan invokes the GitHub API (`PATCH /repos/navapbc/rebar`) and `gh` CLI (`gh api …`, `gh label list`) but never states the auth preconditions: the PATCH call requires a token with `repo` (or `admin:repo`) scope held by a repository admin, and the `gh` CLI must be authenticated against that identity. Without specifying this, an implementer who runs `gh api repos/navapbc/rebar --jq .has_issues` with a read-only token will silently get `false` even after attempting the flip — the AC proof step can produce a misleading result.
findings.location: What section ('Flip `has_issues=true`') and AC1 ('prove: `gh api repos/navapbc/rebar --jq .has_issues` returns `true`')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Prerequisites subsection under 'What' or 'Acceptance Criteria': 'Auth precondition: the implementer (or automation token) must be a repository admin with a `gh` session authenticated via `gh auth login` using a token with `repo` scope. Verify with `gh auth status` before running the flip or any AC proof commands.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
